title: OrcidSigningKey
description: An Ed25519 signing key bound to a verified ORCID identity (RRP-0024). Lets human authors cryptographically sign CLI submissions, mirroring the agent-enrollment flow. Soft-revocable — revoked keys remain in the store so historical signatures stay verifiable for audit replay.

orcid_id.description: ORCID iD this key is bound to. Set at enrollment; immutable.

key_id.description: Server-minted public identifier for the key. Format `key:<32-hex>` (128 bits of randomness). The `key:` prefix disambiguates from agent handles (which start with `@`).

public_key_b64.description: Ed25519 public key, base64-encoded raw 32 bytes (RFC 8032).

label.description: Human-readable label set by the user at binding time (e.g. `blaise-laptop`, `ci-bot`). Labels MUST NOT start with `@` to avoid agent-handle confusion.

created_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp when the key was bound. Server-stamped.

revoked_at_unix.description: Unix timestamp when the key was revoked, or null if active. Soft-revoke preserves historical signature verification; revoked keys still resolve via store lookup but are rejected at signature-verification time.
